msgs.text:
Turn 27 — the first 2-ghost obstruction dies I continued from the attached statement and history. The problem is still the over-G isomorphism question for the profinite one-relator quotients ΓS,ΓH, with F=P⋊G and relators rS,rH as defined in the statement.The current obstruction is not the residual mod-2 tower — that tower was already closed by the Cm=Lm induction — but the finite coefficient lift over the local Ef-coefficient ring.

The result of this turn is:λ8((K4I9 ∩kerg4(2))[2])=F2.

So the first A2=R/(4,t) coefficient ghost obstruction does not survive.Recall the Turn 26 reduction. Locally R=EfΛ≃Z2 [[t]], t=s−1, and after the H1 -change the dangerous row is(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t).ThusA2=R/(4,t):(s2+1,s−1)=(2,0),so the 2-ghost records only the Y0 -coordinate of a lower degree-4 seed. Turn 26 reduced the whole first 2-coefficient test to the question whether some seed inK4I9 ∩kerg4(2)still toggles the missing degree-16 functional λ.

Use the degree-4 restricted basise0e1e2e3e4e5=in4 (X,(X,C))=AAAB+AABA+ABAA+BAAA,=in4 (Y,(X,C))=AABB+BBAA,=in4 (Y,(Y,C))=ABBB+BABB+BBAB+BBBA,=in4(C2)=(AB+BA)2,=A4,=B4.These are the same actual restricted degree-4 representatives used earlier; this matters because naked associative degree-4 polynomials miss forced Bockstein tails.

A degree-4 lower seed is a triple(u0,u1,uq)∈L43,meaning postmultiplication of Y0,Y1,q, respectively. Starting from the Turn-5/Turn-8 I9 -base liftS(Y0(8),Y1(8),q(8))≡X2 (modI9),I recomputed the degree-4 survivor space using actual restricted representatives and allowing continuation by degree 5,6,7 corrections. This matches the earlier structural numbers: the first-order 3L4→T5 map has rank 6, the continuation map through degrees 6,7,8 has rank 60, and the surviving degree-4 seeds form an 8-dimensional linear space K4I9. Turn 13 had already found this 8-dimensional survivor space and the 28=256 extendable degree-4 seeds.

A convenient basis of K4I9is:(e5,0,0),(e0+e4, e0+e1+e3, 0),(e0,0,e0),(e1,0,e1),(e2,0,e2),(e3,0,e3),(e4,0,e4),(0,0,e5).

Sinceg4(2)(u0,u1,uq)=u0,the zero-2-ghost subspace isK4I9 ∩kerg4(2)=⟨(0, e0+e1+e3, e0+e4), (0,0,e5)⟩.

In particular, the seed η:=(0,0,e5)is available. In group language, this is simply q⟼qY4,with Y0 and Y1 unchanged at degree 4. It has zero 2-ghost, because its Y0 -component is zero.

But its square shadow ise5[2]=(B4)[2]=B8.

The missing root functional from Turns 11–14 isλˉ8 (W)=coeff (AB+BA)4 (W)+coeffB8(W),soλˉ8(e5[2])=λˉ8(B8)=1.

Thereforeλˉ8((K4I9 ∩kerg4(2))[2])=F2.This answers the Turn 26 test positively. The old Y0↦Y0Y4 seed is blocked by the 2-ghost because it contributes 2B4, but the section-cocycle seed q↦qY4 supplies the same B8 square shadow while remaining invisible to g4(2). Turn 14 had shown that the lower Y4 -shadow toggles the degree-16 λ-class; Turn 27 shows that the same missing λ-direction is reachable inside the zero-2-ghost kernel.

So the first coefficient layer over

R/(4,t)does not produce a finite-coefficient obstruction. Together with Turn 26’s observation that the t-ghost is harmless at this bottleneck, both first coefficient generators 2 and t=s−1 are absorbed at the degree-16 lower-base obstruction point.The structural lesson is broader than this one seed. The q-coordinate is a section-cocycle coordinate. At the A2 -level, the lower 2-ghost only sees the Y0 -coordinate, but the square transgression still sees q. Thus the section-cocycle direction is not merely auxiliary; it is a ghost-null source of square shadows. This is exactly the kind of uniform pattern needed for the coefficient-Bockstein induction, rather than another isolated tail computation.This still does not prove the full over-G isomorphism. The remaining task is to promote this first-layer observation to an m=(2,t)-adic induction: at every coefficient layer, the kernel of the relevant lower ghost must still have enough section/Nielsen square-shadow freedom to hit the next restricted-square quotient. The compactness framework will then close once finite coefficient nonemptiness is established for all coefficient-and-degree truncations.

ESTABLISHED: The first A2=R/(4,t) coefficient ghost obstruction at the degree-16 bottleneck dies. Explicitly,K4I9 ∩kerg4(2)has a seed (0,0,e5), i.e. q↦qY4, with zero 2-ghost and nonzero missing square shadow:λˉ8((B4)[2])=λˉ8(B8)=1.

GAP: This clears the first 2-coefficient layer, but not yet the full finite coefficient nonemptiness lemma over all Artinian quotients of R≃Z2 [[t]].

NEXT: Formulate the uniform coefficient induction: prove that at each m-adic layer, the zero-lower-ghost correction kernel still maps onto the required square-shadow quotient, with the section-cocycle q-coordinate supplying the ghost-null square transgressions.[TURN27-FIRST-2-GHOST-CLEARED-BY-Q-SEED]
